title: Rotavirus deaths prevented by vaccination
subtitle: The number of [rotavirus](#dod:rotavirus) deaths of children under five years old that were prevented in 2016 due to the rotavirus vaccine.
caption:
Rotavirus deaths prevented by vaccination, 2016

The number of rotavirus deaths of children under five years old that were prevented in 2016 due to the rotavirus vaccine.

This choropleth map displays the estimated number of rotavirus deaths prevented among children under five in 2016, using a color scale ranging from pale yellow for zero prevented deaths to dark blue for values exceeding 3,000. The data shows a distinct geographic concentration of high impact in sub-Saharan Africa and parts of South and East Asia. The darkest blue band, representing more than 3,000 prevented deaths, covers Nigeria, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Ethiopia, and China. India and Brazil appear in the next tier, falling within the 1,000 to 3,000 range. A broad middle band of teal and medium blue, indicating between 100 and 1,000 prevented deaths, spans much of the rest of the African continent, including countries like Tanzania and Kenya, as well as Mexico, Indonesia, and the Philippines. Lighter shades of green, corresponding to 10 to 100 prevented deaths, are visible in nations such as Argentina, South Africa, and Egypt. Large portions of the map, including the United States, Canada, Australia, and most of Europe, are shaded in pale yellow, indicating that fewer than 10 deaths were prevented in these regions. Areas marked with diagonal hatching, such as Greenland and Western Sahara, denote locations where no data is available. The visual distribution highlights that the highest absolute numbers of prevented deaths coincide with regions possessing large populations of young children and significant burdens of rotavirus disease.
